creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.
contentSnippet: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

creator: Sean O'Kane
title: (No AI) SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
content: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.
contentSnippet: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

title: (No AI) AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![An AI-generated image of a Trojan soldier.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-14-at-3.55.09-PM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of [_The Odyssey_](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/849026/the-trailer-for-christopher-nolans-the-odyssey-dropped-this-morning)_._ It's on track to [rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million](https://deadline.com/2026/06/the-odyssey-box-office-opening-projection-1236968681/) in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see [how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech](https://www.theverge.com/news/668287/christopher-nolan-odyssey-imax-film-100-percent) to make the Homeric classic feel fresh. But another director is trying to capitalize on the buzz around Nolan's project to drum up interest in an Odysseus-focused movie of his own.

On Tuesday, [film studio Fountain 0 announced](https://watch.fountain0.com/movies/odysseus-the-fall) that it is working on an AI-generated reimagining of _The Odyssey_ titled _Odysseus: The Fall_, which will be availabl …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/965616/ash-koosha-odysseus-the-fall-foundtain-zero-tilly-norwood)

This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. But another director is trying to capitalize on the buzz around Nolan's project to drum up interest in an Odysseus-focused movie of his own.
On Tuesday, film studio Fountain 0 announced that it is working on an AI-generated reimagining of The Odyssey titled Odysseus: The Fall, which will be availabl …

title: (No AI) Suno snatched millions of songs from YouTube, Genius, and Deezer
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A smiling computer surrounded by music notes connected like data points.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25383554/STK467_AI_MUSIC_CVirginia_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

So when is fair use actually just stealing? | Cath Virginia / The Verge | Photo from Getty Images

Suno data obtained in a hacking incident has exposed that the AI music generator was trained by scraping millions of songs and lyrics from online audio platforms, including YouTube Music, Deezer, and Genius, [_404 Media_](https://www.404media.co/hack-reveals-suno-ai-music-generator-scraped-youtube-deezer-and-genius/) reports. Given that Suno has avoided revealing what's in its training datasets and how they were acquired, this a rare glimpse into what Suno has _actually_ been taking from online platforms.

That's relevant because Suno has been the subject of several lawsuits that allege it used [copyrighted materials](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/906896/sunos-copyright-ai-music-covers) to train its AI models. In [a notable case](https://www.theverge.com/2024/6/24/24184710/riaa-ai-lawsuit-suno-udio-copyright-umg-sony-warner) filed by the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA), [Suno openly admitted](https://www.theverge.com/2024/8/2/24211842/ai-music-riaa-copyright-lawsuit-suno-udio-fair-use) th …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/966072/suno-ai-music-training-scraping-youtube-hack)
contentSnippet:
So when is fair use actually just stealing? | Cath Virginia / The Verge | Photo from Getty Images	

Suno data obtained in a hacking incident has exposed that the AI music generator was trained by scraping millions of songs and lyrics from online audio platforms, including YouTube Music, Deezer, and Genius, 404 Media reports. Given that Suno has avoided revealing what's in its training datasets and how they were acquired, this a rare glimpse into what Suno has actually been taking from online platforms.
That's relevant because Suno has been the subject of several lawsuits that allege it used copyrighted materials to train its AI models. In a notable case filed by the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA), Suno openly admitted th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
content: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
contentSnippet: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

title: (No AI) GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.
contentSnippet: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) Thinking Machines Lab Drops Its First Model
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.
contentSnippet: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.
